app.auth.verifyingBody: Authenticating with the saved access key...
app.auth.inputRequired: Please enter the admin access key
app.auth.securityItem1: The admin console uses ADMIN_ACCESS_KEY; it falls back to PROXY_ACCESS_KEY if unset.
app.auth.securityItem2: The key is stored locally and will be reused for automatic sign-in.
app.auth.securityItem3: Do not share your access key with others.
app.auth.securityItem4: Change the server config immediately if you suspect a leak.
app.auth.securityItem5: After {attempts} failed attempts, access is locked for 5 minutes.
cockpit.overrideActive: Custom channel order is active. Auto-reset in {time}
cockpit.overrideActivePerpetual: Custom channel order active (will not expire until manually restored)
cockpit.empty: No active flights. Conversations will appear on radar when requests pass through the gateway.
cockpit.noMatches: No conversations match the current filter.
cockpit.tooltip.pausedChannel: Channel is paused and skipped by scheduling
cockpit.tooltip.circuitOpen: Circuit breaker is open; this channel is temporarily skipped

cockpit.tooltip.moveChannelToTop: Move {name} to the top of the channel order
cockpit.tooltip.demoteChannel: Move {name} to the end of the channel order

capability.noModelsFound: No models found. Add models manually below.
capability.noApiKeyError: This channel has no API key configured and cannot run capability tests.
orchestration.title: Channel orchestration
orchestration.dragHint: Drag to change priority, saved automatically
orchestration.enableFromPool: Enable channels from the standby pool below
orchestration.appendToFailover: Once enabled, it will be appended to the end of the failover sequence
orchestration.promotionSuccess: Channel {name} is now top priority and will be preferred for the next 5 minutes
orchestration.resumeSuccessWithKeys: Channel resumed, metrics reset, and {count} blacklisted key(s) restored
orchestration.deleteActiveGuard: Cannot delete: keep at least one active channel in the failover sequence
orchestration.cacheWriteHighHint: The cache write ratio is high in the last 15 minutes, which may indicate suboptimal cache-hit configuration.
schedulerDiagnose.title: Scheduler diagnose
addChannel.quickInputHint: Paste configuration text containing Base URL and API Key, the system will parse automatically
addChannel.quickInputPlaceholder:
Paste config snippet to auto-detect Base URL and API Key (multi-line supported)
https://api.example.com
https://api-backup.example.com
sk-ant-api03-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
sk-ant-api03-yyyyyyyyyyyyyyyy
addChannel.enterApiKey: Enter at least one API key
copilotOAuth.description: Use GitHub OAuth to authorize Copilot. After authorization, the GitHub OAuth token is added as a key for this channel. This token is only used to communicate with GitHub; CCX automatically exchanges it for a short-lived Copilot token.
copilotOAuth.success: Authorization succeeded. The GitHub OAuth token has been added to this channel.
copilotOAuth.expired: The authorization code has expired. Please restart authorization.
copilotOAuth.quickAddKeyHint: No pasted API key is required. Sign in with GitHub Copilot after creating the channel.
copilotOAuth.quickAddHint: A GitHub Copilot channel does not need a pasted API key. After creating it, open Edit channel → Authentication and click "Sign in with GitHub Copilot" to authorize. The Base URL defaults to https://api.githubcopilot.com.
addChannel.streamTimeoutDescription: Configure timeout parameters for streaming responses
addChannel.streamFirstByteTimeoutHint: Maximum time to wait for the first response content
addChannel.streamChunkIntervalTimeoutHin: Maximum interval between two data chunks
addChannel.streamOverallTimeoutHint: Maximum duration for the entire streaming response
addChannel.baseUrlPlaceholder:
One URL per line, supports multiple Base URLs
Example:
https://api.openai.com/v1
https://api2.openai.com/v1
addChannel.modelHint: Configure source model to target model mapping here.
addChannel.modelHintTip: Click a target model field to load supported upstream models. Detection status for each API key appears in the key list.
addChannel.sourceModelPlaceholder: Select or enter a source model
addChannel.fastModeHint: Codex /fast mode speeds up generation on supported models for faster coding iteration, but consumes credits or usage limits at a higher rate
addChannel.reasoningParamStyleHint: Choose whether OpenAI-style upstream requests use reasoning.effort or reasoning_effort.
addChannel.supportedModelsPlaceholder: Press Enter after each rule, for example gpt-4*, *image*, or !*image*
addChannel.supportedModelsHint: Matches the original model requested by the client before model redirect is applied. Leave empty to allow all models. Supported forms: exact, prefix*, *suffix, *contains*, and exclusion with !. Vectors embedding compatibility is matched after modelMapping.
addChannel.supportedModelsInvalidPattern: Invalid rule ignored. Only exact, prefix*, *suffix, *contains*, and ! exclusion are allowed.
addChannel.defaultContextWindowHint: Used when the actual model is not covered by the built-in registry or JSON overrides. Empty uses the unknown-model policy.
addChannel.defaultMaxOutputHint: Only filters requests that explicitly set an output limit.
addChannel.modelCapabilitiesJsonHint: Keys use the actual model name after model redirect and may use wildcards. Empty object means no built-in override.
addChannel.modelCapabilitiesJsonInvalid: Enter a valid JSON object. Values may only include contextWindowTokens, maxOutputTokens, thinkingMode, and reasoningEfforts.
addChannel.allowUnknownContextLabel: Allow unknown models for large context
addChannel.allowUnknownContextHint: By default, unknown-capability models are candidates only up to 200K tokens. Enable this to allow larger requests.
addChannel.apiKeyRequired: At least one key is required
addChannel.apiKeyLoadBalance: Multiple keys can be added as failover candidates
addChannel.skipTlsHint: Enable temporarily only for self-signed certificates or domain mismatch. Keep disabled in production.
addChannel.lowQualityHint: When enabled, token usage is force-estimated locally and the local value is used if the deviation exceeds 5%.
addChannel.autoBlacklistBalanceHint: Automatically move the key to the blacklist when the upstream reports insufficient balance.
addChannel.codexNativeToolPassthroughHin: Convert Codex native tools (apply_patch, namespace, etc.) to OpenAI function format in passthrough mode, so upstream models can invoke them.
addChannel.codexToolCompatHint: Enable Codex CLI compatibility: strip client-only tools for Responses passthrough upstreams, or convert them to function proxy tools for Chat/Claude/Gemini upstreams.
addChannel.stripImageGenerationToolHint: Remove image_generation and the Codex image_gen namespace to avoid upstream permission errors when image generation is not enabled.
addChannel.convertImageUrlToB64JsonHint: When the client requests response_format=b64_json but the upstream only returns a URL, download the image and convert it to b64_json.
addChannel.normalizeMetadataUserIdHint: Auto-convert JSON object user_id to a flat string for upstream compatibility.
addChannel.stripBillingHeaderHint: Remove cch= billing parameters from system text blocks before forwarding, only for the current Messages channel.
addChannel.stripEmptyTextBlocksHint: Remove bare empty text content blocks before forwarding, for Claude-protocol upstreams that strictly reject Claude Code tool-use placeholders.
addChannel.normalizeSystemRoleToTopLevel: For Opus 4.8 / Fable 5 / similar new clients that emit system as a message role: extract them back to the top-level system field before forwarding, for legacy Claude upstreams that only accept user/assistant roles.
addChannel.normalizeNonstandardChatRoles: Converts non-standard roles like developer to user before forwarding upstream. Recommended for domestic Chinese models that lack non-standard role support.
addChannel.stripCodexClientToolsHint: Enable only for legacy Responses upstreams that reject Codex CLI 0.130+ tool schemas.
addChannel.injectDummyThoughtSignatureHi: Injects a dummy signature into functionCall for third-party APIs that require this field. Disable it for the official API.
addChannel.stripThoughtSignatureHint: Removes thought_signature from functionCall for older Gemini APIs that do not support it.
addChannel.noVisionHint: When enabled, requests with images will skip this channel and failover to the next
addChannel.visionDisabled: This model does not support image input
addChannel.visionFallbackPlaceholder: Model to use when request contains images
addChannel.visionFallbackHint: When a request contains images and the target model does not support vision, this model will be used instead. Leave empty to failover to the next channel.
addChannel.visionFallbackReasoningHint: Reasoning effort used when image requests switch to the vision fallback model.
addChannel.historicalImageTurnLimitHint: Images in historical turns beyond this limit are replaced with [Image] placeholder to avoid unnecessary vision fallback. 0 means unlimited; valid range is 2-10 when set.
addChannel.compactModelPlaceholder: Leave empty to use original model
addChannel.compactModelHint: Upstream model name for local compact (compression/summary), bypassing model mapping. Always used for non-Responses protocol channels; for Responses channels, used as fallback when native compact fails.
addChannel.passbackReasoningContentHint: Convert thinking blocks to reasoning_content when forwarding to Claude-protocol upstreams that require OpenAI-style reasoning_content (e.g. mimo).
addChannel.passbackThinkingBlocksHint: Project real reasoning_content into Claude content[].thinking when forwarding to strict Claude thinking-mode upstreams (e.g. DeepSeek/GLM).
addChannel.customHeadersHint: Add or override HTTP headers sent to the upstream service.
addChannel.rpmHint: Only affects capability tests; higher RPM makes tests finish faster.
addChannel.proxyUrlHint: Supports HTTP/HTTPS/SOCKS5 proxy URLs for accessing upstream services through a proxy.
addChannel.requestTimeoutMsHint: Only applies to non-streaming upstream requests. Leave empty to inherit global REQUEST_TIMEOUT.
addChannel.requestTimeoutMsInvalid: Enter an integer from 1 to 300 seconds, or leave empty to inherit the global timeout.
addChannel.responseHeaderTimeoutMsHint: Maximum wait for upstream HTTP response headers. Relax only for slow-start channels such as local inference.
addChannel.responseHeaderTimeoutMsInvali: Enter an integer from 1 to 300 seconds, or leave empty to inherit the global timeout.
addChannel.rateLimitSectionHint: Proactively throttle requests before they reach the upstream to avoid 429 errors (ideal for free / low-quota providers)
addChannel.rateLimitRpmHint: Max requests per minute sent upstream for this channel; excess requests queue. 0 or empty = no limit.
addChannel.rateLimitWindowMinutesHint: Sliding window duration in minutes. Example: RPM=50 + window=5 means "max 50 requests per 5 minutes".
addChannel.rateLimitBurstHint: Token bucket size for short bursts. Defaults to the RPM value when empty.
addChannel.rateLimitMaxConcurrentHint: Max in-flight upstream requests; excess requests queue. 0 or empty = no limit.
addChannel.rateLimitAutoFromHeadersHint: Parse upstream Retry-After / x-ratelimit-* headers and apply dynamic cooldown to further avoid 429.
addChannel.streamTimeoutOverrideHint: Custom value overrides the global stream timeout for this channel.
addChannel.streamTimeoutInheritHint: Disabled means this channel inherits the global stream timeout.
addChannel.routePrefixHint: Access this channel via /{prefix}/v1/messages. Multiple channels can share the same prefix.
addChannel.modelMappingHintChat: Configure model name mapping to redirect the requested model to a target model. Example: redirect "gpt-4o" to "gpt-5.4".
addChannel.modelMappingHintGemini: Configure model name mapping to redirect the requested model to a target model. Example: redirect "gemini-pro" to "gemini-3.5-flash".
addChannel.modelMappingHintResponses: Configure model name mapping to redirect the requested model to a target model. Example: redirect "opus" to "gpt-5.4".
addChannel.modelMappingHintVectors: Configure Embeddings model name mapping to redirect the requested vector model to a target model. Example: redirect "text-embedding-3-small" to "jina-embeddings-v2-base-zh".
addChannel.modelMappingHintMessages: Configure model name mapping to redirect the requested model to a target model. Example: redirect "opus" to "claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929".
addChannel.sourceModelNameTooLong: Custom model name is too long (max 50 characters)
addChannel.sourceModelInvalidChars: Model name can only contain letters, numbers, hyphens, underscores, dots, slashes, colons, @, and +

addChannel.fillBaseUrlAndApiKey: Fill in Base URL and at least one API key first
addChannel.allApiKeysModelsFailed: No API key could load the model list. Check the error shown for each API key.

tooltip.billingEnabled: Enabled: automatically strips cch= billing parameters from system.
tooltip.billingDisabled: Disabled: keeps full billing header information.
tooltip.fuzzyEnabled: Fuzzy mode is enabled: errors are handled loosely and all channels are retried.
tooltip.fuzzyDisabled: Fuzzy mode is disabled: upstream responses are passed through precisely.

dialog.circuitBreaker.title: Tuning Bench
dialog.circuitBreaker.description: Fine-tune circuit breaker and stream control parameters. Changes take effect immediately.
update.title: System Update
update.upToDate: You are running the latest version
update.available: A new version is available. Please download from GitHub Releases.
guide.title: Getting Started
guide.welcome.title: Welcome to CCX
guide.welcome.body: CCX is a multi-upstream AI proxy and protocol-conversion gateway. Get started in three steps:
guide.welcome.step1: Pick a protocol tab at the top
guide.welcome.step2: Add a channel and fill in your API key
guide.welcome.step3: Point your client at the gateway and start using it
guide.protocol.title: Switch protocol at the top
guide.protocol.body1: The tabs at the top — Claude / Codex / Gemini, and more — each manage their own set of channels.
guide.protocol.body2: Switching a tab changes both the channel list below and what the Add channel button creates.
guide.protocol.body3: Pick the protocol your client speaks first.
guide.addChannel.title: Add a channel
guide.addChannel.body1: Click the Add channel button at the top left to add an upstream for the current protocol.
guide.addChannel.body2: Just paste a Base URL and API key — CCX detects the rest automatically.
guide.channelList.title: Reading the channel list
guide.channelList.intro: Each row is a channel. The example below shows a normal channel and a paused one:
guide.channelList.clickName: Click the channel name or the key-count badge to open the channel editor.
guide.channelList.clickRow: Click the middle of the row to expand the usage stats chart.
guide.channelList.clickLogs: Click the log button on the right to view that channel’s logs.
guide.channelList.clickResume: Click the resume button (shown on paused or tripped channels) to restore the channel right away.
guide.channelList.drag: Drag the handle on the left to reorder priority and the failover sequence.

channelEditor.auth.keys.label: API Keys
channelEditor.auth.apiKeyRequired: At least one API key is required
volcengineAccessKey.title: Volcengine control-plane access key
volcengineAccessKey.hint: Bind each ark- inference key to an Access Key ID and Secret Access Key from the same Volcengine account. CCX detects Agent Plan or Coding Plan automatically. Secrets are never returned.
volcengineAccessKey.clearConfirm: Remove the Volcengine access key binding for this inference key?
volcengineAccessKey.accountNotFound: The managed Volcengine account was not found
deepseekBalance.title: DeepSeek account balance
deepseekBalance.hint: Queries the official balance API with the managed account keys already stored in CCX. Keys are never returned.
mimoConsoleCookie.title: MiMo Token Plan console cookie
mimoConsoleCookie.hint: Paste the complete cookie for platform.xiaomimimo.com. CCX validates the session, plan, quota, and associated key. The cookie stays local and is never returned.
mimoConsoleCookie.clearConfirm: Remove the saved MiMo console cookie for this key?
mimoConsoleCookie.accountNotFound: The managed MiMo account was not found
mimoConsoleCookie.keyMismatchConfirm: The cookie key ({cookieKey}) differs from the current key ({currentKey}). Adopt the cookie key and replace it across every protocol route in this account?
compshareConsoleCookie.title: CompShare plan quota
compshareConsoleCookie.hint: Paste the complete CompShare console cookie. CCX derives the user, project, and CSRF context and only binds a plan containing the current key. The cookie stays local and is never returned.
compshareConsoleCookie.clearConfirm: Remove the saved CompShare console cookie for this key?
compshareConsoleCookie.accountNotFound: The managed CompShare account was not found
kimiConsoleToken.title: Kimi Code plan usage
kimiConsoleToken.hint: Paste the access_token from the kimi.com session (Bearer or the full Authorization value is accepted). The token stays in the local configuration and is not returned by the bind, refresh, or account-list APIs. Because the usage APIs do not identify API keys, make sure the login owns this Kimi Code key.
kimiConsoleToken.clearConfirm: Remove the saved Kimi session token for this key?
kimiConsoleToken.accountNotFound: The managed Kimi account was not found
channelEditor.mapping.redirect.title: Model Redirect
channelEditor.mapping.configured.label: Configured
channelEditor.mapping.source.label: Source model
channelEditor.mapping.target.label: Target model
channelEditor.advanced.authHeader.label: Auth header
channelEditor.advanced.authHeader.hint: Override how the upstream API key is sent; Auto keeps the existing compatibility logic
channelEditor.compat.title: Compatibility
channelEditor.compat.fastMode.label: Fast mode
channelEditor.compat.fastMode.hint: Codex /fast mode speeds up generation on supported models for faster coding iteration, but consumes credits or usage limits at a higher rate
channelEditor.compat.noVision.label: Skip image requests
channelEditor.compat.noVision.hint: When enabled, requests containing images skip this channel and failover to the next one
channelEditor.compat.reasoningStyle.hint: Choose whether to use reasoning.effort or reasoning_effort for OpenAI-style requests
channelEditor.compat.passbackReasoning.h: Convert historical thinking blocks into OpenAI-style reasoning_content for passback. Claude-protocol upstreams that require reasoning_content (e.g. MiMo, DeepSeek and other Chinese first-party APIs) need this on; standard Anthropic endpoints or full-featured relays usually don't. Run Diagnose to auto-detect.
channelEditor.compat.passbackThinking.hi: Project real reasoning_content back into content[].thinking. Upstreams that strictly validate Claude thinking blocks (e.g. DeepSeek, GLM and other Chinese first-party APIs) need this on; standard Anthropic endpoints or full-featured relays usually don't. Run Diagnose to auto-detect.
channelEditor.compat.stripThoughtSignatu: Remove the thought_signature field from functionCall
channelEditor.compat.injectDummySignatur: Inject a dummy signature into functionCall for third-party APIs
channelEditor.compat.stripEmptyBlocks.hi: Remove the bare empty text block Claude Code inserts before tool_use. Strict first-party APIs of Chinese models (e.g. DeepSeek, GLM) often return 400 because of it and usually need this on; standard Anthropic endpoints or lenient relays usually don't. Run Diagnose to auto-detect.
channelEditor.compat.normalizeSystem.hin: Extract system messages from the messages array back to the top-level system field. Native first-party APIs of Chinese models (DeepSeek, Kimi, GLM, MiniMax, etc.) often accept only user/assistant roles and usually need this on; standard Anthropic endpoints or full-featured relays usually don't. Run Diagnose to auto-detect.
channelEditor.compat.codexNativeTools.hi: Convert Codex native tools into OpenAI function format
channelEditor.compat.codexCompat.label: Codex tool compatibility
channelEditor.compat.stripImageGen.label: Strip image generation tool
channelEditor.compat.stripImageGen.hint: Remove image generation tool calls from requests
channelEditor.compat.convertImageUrlToB6: Download and return base64 data when the client asks for b64_json but the upstream only returns a URL
channelEditor.compat.historicalImageLimi: 0 means unlimited; when set to 2-10, images beyond the limit are replaced with placeholder
channelEditor.compat.diagnoseFailed: Diagnosis failed, please check channel config
channelEditor.runtime.title: Runtime
channelEditor.runtime.autoBlacklist.hint: Automatically trip/disable keys with balance errors
channelEditor.runtime.autoLearnRateLimit: Parse Retry-After / x-ratelimit-* headers and adjust cooldown dynamically
channelEditor.transport.title: Transport
channelEditor.transport.proxyUrl.label: Proxy URL
channelEditor.transport.requestTimeout.h: Maximum time to wait for upstream response
channelEditor.transport.responseHeaderTi: Wait after connection for upstream HTTP headers; relax per slow-start channel only
channelEditor.transport.skipTls.label: Skip TLS verification
channelEditor.transport.skipTls.hint: Disable TLS certificate verification for upstream
channelEditor.transport.lowQuality.label: Low quality channel
channelEditor.transport.lowQuality.hint: Mark this channel as low quality, used as last resort
channelEditor.rateLimit.title: Rate Limit
channelEditor.rateLimit.rpm.label: Max requests per minute (RPM)
channelEditor.rateLimit.window.label: Sliding window (s)
channelEditor.streamTimeout.title: Stream Timeout
channelEditor.streamTimeout.overrideHint: Custom value overrides the global stream timeout for this channel
channelEditor.streamTimeout.inheritHint: Disabled means this channel inherits the global stream timeout
channelEditor.timeout.title: Timeout strategy
channelEditor.timeout.hint: All values are in seconds; disabled controls inherit global settings
addChannel.modelListEmptyHint: No model candidates yet. Fill in Base URL and API key, then focus a model input to load /models automatically.
addChannel.modelCapabilitiesRowsHint: Model redirect targets are added here automatically with built-in presets when available. You can still add passthrough or unmapped models manually.
addChannel.modelCapabilityModelPlacehold: Select or enter an upstream model
addChannel.modelCapabilitiesRowsInvalid: Check model capability rows. Model names are required; token and price values must be non-negative.
addChannel.embeddingCompatibilityHint: Keys use the actual upstream model name after model redirect. Fallback only stays within the same embeddingSpaceId/actual model, dimensions, and normalization semantics.
addChannel.embeddingCapabilityModelPlace: Select or enter an upstream embedding model
addChannel.embeddingSpaceIdPlaceholder: Empty uses the actual model name
addChannel.embeddingCapabilitiesRowsInva: Check embedding compatibility rows. Actual model is required; dimensions and supported dimensions must be positive integers.

channelDiscovery.hint: Probe models, protocols, redirects, and compatibility switches with the current Base URL and API key.
channelDiscovery.running: Config discovery is still running. Closing this dialog keeps the current task state.
channelDiscovery.reasoningNote: Reasoning effort is a role-based default recommendation. Discovery does not test every effort level.
channelDiscovery.applied: Discovery recommendation applied. Review and save.

subscription.field.name: Display Name

subscription.copilotComingSoon: GitHub Copilot quick access is coming soon. Please configure it manually in Channels for now.

subscription.manageInChannels: Connected subscriptions can be managed centrally on the Channels page.

cockpitOverview.noTodoItems: No to-do items. All endpoints are running normally.
cockpitOverview.empty: No data yet. Please configure channels and subscriptions first.

manualIntent.field.name: Name (optional)
manualIntent.validation.sessionIdRequire: Session pin requires a session ID
autopilot.title: Smart Routing Autopilot
autopilot.modeDesc.off: Smart routing is completely disabled. No traces are computed or recorded.
autopilot.modeDesc.shadow: Only computes and records routing decisions without affecting real scheduling (recommended).
autopilot.modeDesc.assist: Assist mode: reorders candidate list but does not change the final selection.
autopilot.modeDesc.auto: Fully automatic mode: filters and reorders, takes over scheduling after meeting admission criteria.
autopilot.modePanel.title: Global Policy
autopilot.modePanel.killSwitchHint: When enabled, forces smart routing off. Takes priority over routing mode selection.
autopilot.modePanel.killSwitchActive: Kill switch is active. Smart routing is forced off. Disable the kill switch first to use other modes.
autopilot.modePanel.confirmMessage: About to switch to {mode} mode. This mode will affect real scheduling. Do you want to continue?
autopilot.readiness.ready: Auto mode meets the rollout gate
autopilot.readiness.collecting: Collecting safety samples for auto mode
autopilot.readiness.lastRollback: At {time}, consecutive SLO regression was detected and auto was downgraded to assist.
autopilot.readiness.reason.insufficient_: Not enough recent and baseline samples
autopilot.costPreferenceDesc.quality_fir: Prioritize high-quality providers, tolerating higher costs.
autopilot.costPreferenceDesc.cost_first: Prioritize low-cost channels, tolerating some quality decrease.
autopilot.costPreferenceDesc.custom: Use custom savings and quality multipliers.
autopilot.diagnose.title: Smart Routing Diagnosis
autopilot.diagnose.hint: Computes task classification, hard constraints, channel ranking, and model mapping previews only. It sends no upstream request and does not change live scheduling.
autopilot.diagnose.noPlan: The current configuration produced no routing plan.
autopilot.diagnose.noCandidates: No channel matches the current protocol, model, and configuration.
autopilot.traceStats.title: Routing Trace Statistics
autopilot.traceTable.title: Routing Decision Traces
autopilot.traceTable.partial: Some records were skipped due to data corruption
autopilot.traceDetail.title: Routing Decision Detail
autopilot.traceDetail.notFound: Record does not exist, has expired, or was not sampled
autopilot.traceDetail.historicalSchema: Historical record (v1 schema), some fields unavailable
autopilot.quickAdd.alreadyAdded: This Base URL is already configured as "{name}". New keys will be added to the existing channel.
autopilot.quickAdd.provider.label: Provider
autopilot.badge.label: Auto
